title: Game Theory Foundations
description: Learn and master the concepts of Game Theory Foundations with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Strategic Decision Making
description: This course develops the mindset and analytical tools needed to think strategically in competitive and cooperative situations.

title: Simultaneous Games
description: This course explores **games where all players make decisions at the same time without knowing the choices of others**, introducing the core analytical framework of classical game theory.

title: Sequential Games
description: This course explores **games where players make decisions one after another**, allowing strategy to evolve over time based on previous actions.

title: Nash Equilibrium
description: This course explores **the central solution concept of game theory—Nash Equilibrium—and how stable strategic outcomes emerge from rational decision-making.**

title: Repeated Games
description: This course explores **how repeated interactions allow cooperation, trust, and reputation to emerge even among self-interested players.**

title: Cooperative Game Theory
description: This course explores **how groups of players cooperate, share resources, and divide rewards in a fair and stable manner.**

title: Auction Theory
description: This course explores **how auctions are designed, how bidders behave strategically, and why auctions are among the most important real-world applications of game theory.**

title: Voting & Social Choice
description: This course explores **how groups make collective decisions and why designing fair voting systems is one of the most fascinating problems in game theory and economics.**

title: Evolutionary Game Theory
description: This course explores **how strategies evolve over time in populations, explaining cooperation, competition, and adaptation in biology, economics, and artificial intelligence.**

title: Mechanism Design
description: This course explores **how to design rules and systems so that individually rational behavior leads to desirable collective outcomes**, often called *reverse game theory*.

title: Applications of Game Theory
description: This capstone course brings together the entire curriculum, showing **how game theory is used to solve real-world problems across science, technology, business, and society.**
